headline: Transcript: Javier Milei scores Argentina economy win
summary: Kasia Broussalian talks to Ciara Nugent and Edward White
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Javier Milei scores Argentina economy win’

Kasia BroussalianGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Wednesday, December 18th. And this is your FT News Briefing. Microsoft is hoarding chips for artificial intelligence, and Argentina’s president has scored a major economic win. Plus, Huawei is trying to expand its reach into electric vehicles. I’m Kasia Broussalian, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Microsoft bought a whole lot of Nvidia chips this year and by that I mean nearly half a million of the company’s current flagship product. It’s something called the Hopper. And that’s at least twice as many chips as any of Microsoft’s competitors like Meta, Amazon or Google. It’s all a big deal because Nvidia’s Hopper is a hot commodity. Demand far outstrips supply. So hoarding it all gives the tech giant an edge in building out the next generation of AI systems.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Ar
